title: Farmhouse Counter Stools
summary: A splayed-leg 2x2 counter stool with a thick 2x10 seat — double-bevel angled legs give it real outward flare with no visible hardware angle jig required.
description:
This stool's legs carry the entire design: cut with a true double compound angle — 5° angle and 5° bevel together, ends parallel — so each leg flares outward in two directions at once from a seat that stays perfectly level. Mark the leg locations on the underside of the seat first, rotating each leg so it's splayed outward with the short point of the leg top toward the inside corner — getting this orientation right before drilling a single hole is what keeps all four legs flaring the same direction.

The two support rows go on at two different heights: measure up from the bottom of the leg on its inside face, first at 7-1/2" for the longer support run, then at 4" for the shorter one — these aren't structural guesses, they're the two rows that keep a splayed-leg stool from racking side to side under someone sitting down at counter height.

Leveling feet are a genuinely worthwhile add-on here, and not just cosmetic: a stool with four independently angled legs is more sensitive to an unlevel floor than a square-legged one, and floor-protecting feet do double duty solving both problems for about fifty cents each.

tools.note: The double compound (5° angle, 5° bevel) leg cuts.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Both ends cut with a double bevel: 5° angle and 5° bevel, ends parallel, longest to shortest point measurement.
Both ends cut at a 5° angle (not a double bevel), ends not parallel, long point to long point.
Both ends cut at a 5° angle (not a double bevel), ends not parallel, long point to long point.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut one seat at 15-3/4" from the 2x10. Cut four legs at 22-5/8", two upper supports at 15-3/8" and two lower supports at 9-1/2" from the 2x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft 2x2. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Mark the leg locations on the underside of the seat, rotating each leg so it's splayed outward with the leg top's short point toward the inside corner. Attach with glue and a screw driven from the top of the seat.

steps.title: Add the upper supports
steps.body: Measure up 7-1/2" on the inside of each leg and mark. Attach the longer supports with one screw per end.

steps.title: Add the lower supports
steps.body: Measure up 4" on the inside of each leg and attach the remaining shorter supports.

steps.title: Add leveling feet and finish
steps.body: Attach leveling feet to help adjust for a slightly out-of-square stool and protect your floors. Sand and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Counter stool with splayed double-compound-angle 2x2 legs
